park_name: NRMA South West Rocks Holiday Park

reviews.review_text: Great family park, with lots of activities for the kids. Staff very helpful and friendly.

Everything was great! Great for kids and lots of activities for them.
Unit ok but no lookout. if you want to sit on your deck you look into other decks and across is more camp sites - we were lucky there was no one camping there.
Location in the bush and lots of kangaroos which was lovely. Positive experience

Probably 40yrs+ since I stayed in a caravan park. I was looking for affordable, no frills accommodation, with self catering facilities in the SWR area, for a short birding venture. As an NRMA member, thought I'd support the park and enjoy the discount.
Location was good for me, but I only cared about proximity to my hikes. I originally booked a 1 bed cabin, but accepted the offer of a free upgrade to 2 bed. Gave me extra living room space and a second bedroom to store my luggage.
My bed was comfortable, cooking facilities & utensils more than adequate. Loved the ceiling fans. HOWEVER, the location of this cabin was not at all suitable for a solo traveller - right in front of the children's water play area, which included periodic emptying/spraying of large water features and much screaming from excited youngsters. I hate to think what it would be like in the summer!
Had a very pleasant 2 night stay in May. Staff lovely, facilities extensive and a secluded spot out of the main town.

In the glamping room with ensuite.
Very nice, quite away from the families
A lovely comfortable place with kangaroos on the grounds lots for kids to do and close to so many things to see and do
A great family experience at this little hidden gem! It was so peaceful and relaxing wandering the grounds and enjoying the waterpark and wildlife with our kids. We’ll be back!

Great location
The shower pressure and temperature is AMAZING!
Our kids love the pool area ita has something for every age.
We love Sputh West Rocks.
Back creek is amazing as the tide is coming in.

Hands down the best park we’ve been to. The  Freshwater Villa (number 28) was in the perfect spot. It was roomy, clean and had everything we needed. We appreciated the plentiful supply of laundry powder, dishwasher powder, garbage bags and cleaning supplies. Makes it easy to keep things clean and fresh. The gardens and surrounds exceptionally tidy and cared for.
The waterpark was great. Mini golf awesome, games room with air hockey, pool table and table tennis provided great afternoon fun.
Friendly staff. Great location and short drive to beaches and local shops and cafes. Highly recommend. Thankyou for a wonderful stay!

Great apot for the family to enjoy a break.
Very clean and lota to do.

This is a great location as it's so close to everything! So we would stay here again.

However, it's very run down & needs some much needed renovation & TLC...
To us this park was quite expensive to stay at ( powered caravan site )

Also to note if you do have kids it's PERFECT! as it's very catered to children & it's a HUGE park!
On night was not enough for enjoy this beautiful place so we rent one more night.I recommand this place thanks for your welcoming

Great park with loads of fun activities for kids!
Safe and clean amenities.
The camp kitchen was so well equipped!
Perfect for families
Very nice holiday park with a lot of activities for active and adventurous children.

Great park for the kids. Went with another couple and their child and our kids absolutely loved the water slides and the quad bikes. Plenty to do we had a game of put put and loads of activities for the kids. We all also enjoyed the jumping pillow. There was lots of wildlife around. Very happy with our stay.

This park is apparently on a swamp and the stink is pure sewer!! Avoid this place and go somewhere else.
Management doesn’t care, it should not be available for rent when this stench is around.  You can’t sleep with the windows open or leave the door open during the day.
No soap or shampoo provided even the worst motels in Australia provide this. Television approximately 24 years old. No discount for nrma membership. Not coming back

Terrific spot, bathrooms were always immaculate. Workers/ staff very polite. Everything is there. Would stay again. For not yet grey nomads ( go mid week) just be aware it's a family park so lots of kids. Movie nights & activities were a bonus.
Love this place. Cafe is fantastic. Amenities are clean. Activities and pool is the BEST!
Not for us retirees.. Too many screaming kids running wild. Great for families i guess.

We booked in on a Tuesday, the park was reasonably quiet. However, that changed on Thursday. The park started to fill up with families and by Friday it was quite busy.

The park is family friendly, with an awesome pool area, tennis, putt putt etc. A text message also advised of a movie night and a disco over the weekend. These days it seems the direction parks are going. A mini resort where families are catered for.

I am not complaining, just a heads up for older couples who might be looking for somewhere a little more peaceful.

The park had an on-site Cafe, where the coffee was very good. The staff in the Cafe and at check in were very friendly and helpful.

We also ate a couple of times at the Cafe, bacon and egg rolls were nice and the supreme pizza on Friday night was very good.

Amenities were kept clean, although a little dated. The wife wasn't impressed by some people and their lack of keeping the their dogs under control. Especially when there are ducks and the odd Kangaroo about. But that's an owner issue, not a park issue.

All in all, a great park close to the old gaol and the lighthouse and short trip into the heart of South West Rocks.

Fantastic park, great value. Clean and well maintained.
One of the best kids clubs we have experienced.
Water park is great for both kids and adults

Fantastic park and facilities especially for families, unfortunately our stay was somewhat ruined by having a worker in our camp-site for almost an entire day cutting hedges, it didn't feel very private having a worker next to my van windows making noise with the hedge cutter, it also made me feel uncomfortable even while in my van and near impossible to sit outside as it was noisy and had branches everywhere. The worker also moved our mat and didn't put it back. Would have been 10 out of 10 if one day wasn't uncomfortable and annoying. Bathrooms are super clean however again I was asked to leave at night whilst showering due to cleaning however the times on the door stated am times not pm so was slightly inconvenient having to get out after being at an event the entire day.
Amazing place for a family holiday. Facilities and staff were great, was paradise for the kids 6-11yo

This place was all about the kids. If we go back, it will be for them. We stayed in a sunshine tent which was a good balance of roughing it and comfort. Especially when we had torrential rain during the night, I was glad to be off the ground with a solid floor. Though the ensuite bathroom is a detached shack behind the tent, where you have to go outside to shower or use the toilet. I saw this as a plus, as it gave the kids a more of a camper experience. Though calling it an "ensuite" is stretching things a bit?
The sunshine tent location was central enough, close to camp kitchens, jumping pillows, the waterpark/pool and the activities hub. Basically the kids loved it. Would I personally choose to stay there, no. The location is neither in close (walking) proximity to the beautiful South West Rocks town or Arakoon Trial Bay front beach. There are other parks that are located near beaches or inlets on the NSW coast that I would prefer over this park, based on location/views alone. Though I can't fault the park on this, as they have their target market and they cater to it well. I'll probably go back there, as my kids loved it.

This is by far the best NRMA park we’ve been to. It’s not too big, lots for the kids to do and very safe for kids to ride their bikes and scooters around together. The pool is a great size, can get quite busy but it’s perfect for kids. The actual location is so nice, feels like you’re really secluded.

What an absolute gem this caravan park is. We drove up from Sydney for just three days and had the best time. We stayed in a cabin, which was immaculate, honestly more like a five-star hotel than a caravan park stay.

The café food was excellent, the calamari was a standout, and we even treated ourselves to cocktails that were both delicious and great value. My only regret is not discovering this place years ago when our daughter was younger, it would have been perfect. This place is truly brilliant for little kids. The staff are very helpful and friendly too!

The location is spot on, waking up to kangaroos in the garden was a real highlight. A fantastic stay all round and I couldn’t recommend it more highly.

Camped for a week there over Christmas on an unpowered site.
It was right next to a creek however we didn't have any problems with mozzies.
We did see goannas and friendly kangaroos pass through.
The park overall is great for kids, the water park/pool area was perfect for my 4 and 6 year old to just go up and down the pirate ship and slides. Jumping pillow and park were equally good, all kept tidy and under cover too!
I didn't like that the pedal buggys were an extra charge, should be included. The kids activity room was good for the youngest ones (under 5), and overall layout was good, although another family room on the other toilet block would be good.
A camp kitchen just next to us was fantastic, sink,kettle and bbq were handy .
This particular unpowered area looked like just an extra area they haven't actually done much work with so there was no grass, dirty sand instead, not ideal. But the biggest disappointment and reason I did 4 stars is because we had a day of rain and some decent downpours, well a large body of water started making its way straight into our campsite, to mitigate this we used some tools to dig a trench to divert water away, otherwise we would of had a very soggy campsite.
Would I recommend? Yes, just confirm what unpowered spot your in, say no if its any of the following - 229,231,232,234.
This is an amazing place to stay. I have already arranged friends to come back. Kids and adults will absolutely fall in love with this spot.

It was our first time staying at the park in November and my family loved it. My 3 kids aged 3, 6 and 8 loved the splash park and didn’t want to leave it the whole time we stayed. We were so impressed with how clean and well maintained everything was, from the pools, the park amenities and especially the cabins. We stayed in a cottage which had the sweetest outdoor space, beautiful trees created shade, and a grassed area for the kids to play. We stayed two nights, but we’ll definitely return again and stay longer.

Great place clean and very welcoming great facilities we were travelling in a motorhome we stayed on a powered site only complaint I have is the site size was on the small size and the bin birds were out of control raiding camp sites and garbage bins... Shop could probably stay open a bit later
Hele mooie camping. Fantastisch kinderzwembad. Zo uitgebreid en mooi heb ik het op geen andere camping gezien en dat voor zo'n relatief kleine camping.

We had 2 families stay in Cottage 2 - Eucalyptus Cottage. The cottage was clean but a little dated and it only has 1 very small bathroom (with a bath) and the 3rd bedroom with bunks had no ceiling fan. The living area could also do with a few more lights. There is also no BBQ which we were a little surprised about.
You'll need to bring extra detergent and a few kitchen utensils (e.g no measuring cups, whisk, no spray oil ). We had an 18month old and  there is direct access to the stairs into the park, I would request Cottage 1 if you have a little one walking as there is a slightly bigger yard and you could potentially put a baby gate in between the walk way just before the stairs. In terms of facilities my 3.5 year old absolutely loved the pool, jumping pillows and the activities. Its super family friendly. We had no issues with noises as you are sort of a little seperate from the park. Overall i would highly recommend. Thanks for having us!!!

It was busy and probably great for little kids. Sites were super tight, water pressure was low. Google did a good job of putting it up as the only option. We found a few other parks in our travels around SWR that would have suited us much better.

A nice holiday park, very child orientated so don't expect to sit and relax around the compact 'water park'. I had a bad fall in the deep pool where there is an 'invisible' step that serves no purpose at all. I caught the step with the side of my foot and ended up with a broken ankle and 3 broken toes and some grazes. So many people came up while  waiting for medical attention (nobody working in the park had any first aid training) had said they never saw the step and had fallen too.  Not a great experience.
We stayed during school holidays which was nice the park held school holiday activities, bring your mozzie spray as they get bad at night

My family had a fantastic stay at the park. Lots to do for the kids, the pool and splash park is great it keeps them entertained. The cabin was clean and presentable. The onsite cafe was great. Highly recommended for families. Will be back
I was a solo traveller and this was clean organised and suitable but for families with kids? Heaven I would think :) wow the facilities were amazing.

Located in a remote location outside of town. Long walk to beach.
The whole place is worn out and dated.
Mens toilets need a deep clean, especially the gross urinal.
Sinks dont drain properly.
In general it is not the standard of an NRMA Park and needs upgrading.
Wont be back.

Great caravan park. Our powered site was nice and clean. The amenities were good but a bit dated and it had a family room but one wasn't enough with the number of families in the park. The pool and waterslides were excellent. They also had a playground and jumping pillow - which were good for the kids. Very close to the town and beaches. Overall a good park.

Unfortunately a very low rating.
With two small children I do not recommend you stay here.
Unfortunately the security is very low. They leave their gates open so anyone comes in and out.
We experienced this first hand where three cars parked next to our camping spot with a group of 9 people getting out (not all guests) they partied well past 11pm.
We spoke to them to keep it down which the group ignored.
The extra friends that were able to just drive in without an access code didn’t leave.
It was a night from hell.
They partied till after 3am with music, excessive drinking, swearing, car doors opening and slamming shut, yelling and vaping.
The camp grounds had one person speak to them once at 11:30pm but they continued after that.

We unfortunately did not sleep which is horrible and like torture when you are a parent with two small children.
It was not safe.

Not good enough.

When families look forward to and save up money to get away and to be treated with such disregard is very disappointing.

Renee and the team made my 40th birthday so easy! We had families from everywhere converging and a million questions and scenarios including a sip and paint and they were all handled and delivered professionally and patiently. It will now be annual event for our families because everything was easy and a true relaxing holiday. Very good value for money here.

Unfortunately a very low rating. ||With two small children I do not recommend you stay here. ||Unfortunately the security is very low. They leave their gates open so anyone comes in and out.||We experienced this first hand where three cars parked next to our camping spot with a group of 9 people getting out (not all guests) they partied well past 11pm.||We spoke to them to keep it down which the group ignored.||The extra friends that were able to just drive in without an access code didn’t leave.||It was a night from hell.||They partied till after 3am with music, excessive drinking, swearing, car doors opening and slamming shut, yelling and vaping.||The camp grounds had one person speak to them once at 11:30pm but they continued after that.||||We unfortunately did not sleep which is horrible and like torture when you are a parent with two small children. ||It was not safe.||||||Not good enough.||||When families look forward to and save up money to get away and to be treated with such disregard is very disappointing.

I lived my holiday at sth West Rocks. We stated in a cabin. We had everything we needed. Upmarket kettle, toaster, stove, dishwasher and washing machine.  Airconditioner was great, the weather was very hot. Once inside we were very cool. The beds were very comfortable and the bedding was great. We asked for extra towels and received them very quickly. The pool and water park were really top notch. There was tennis,  put put golf and peddle cars. So much to do in the park. So close to the beaches and bowling down the road.

We had a fantastic stay at this campsite! The facilities were well maintained, with loads of fun activities and things to do for the kids. What really made our stay unforgettable, though, was the amazing team on site. We had a slight hiccup with our travel plans, and their support went above and beyond anything we expected. They were genuinely caring, understanding, and accommodating. Thank you for turning what could have been a stressful moment into something positive. We’ll definitely be back!
Great amenities and beautiful surroundings.

The clamping tents were not much more than a tent on a site. No TV, bed linen that smelt, not even a PowerPoint near the bed to plug in required sleep supports. Very overpriced for what we received. We booked for three, room was full of fold up bed and linen on the lounge. Only option was to sit outside with the bugs at night. No Air conditioning, so basic for the price. Certainly not glamping. . Other campers were very loud to after 10.30pm. Overall a disappointing selection.
We loved our stay at South West Rocks, dog friendly, good site size and location within an easy 30 minute stroll into town, walking, cycling and getting around was so easy.
Fantastic family resort with awesome facilities. The pirate water park is amazing! Well maintained park with nice staff. Very green grounds & plenty of kangaroos.
Clean warm great experience
Comfortable bed and well equipped kitchen.

Well maintained park with loads of entertainment for the kids, large sites, great location close to beaches, trial Bay gaol and the Lighthouse, well secured, friendly service. The let down was the showers, probably the worst I have experienced at a park for a while and the Water park and pool was closed during our stay. The opening was delayed because of poor weather which is completely understandable, however it would have been nice to have been told it was closed when we booked. Also its a very expensive park when you consider that it's main attraction was closed.
I would definitely recommend this park to anyone who enjoys the parks with all the bells and whistles, particularly families with young kids. You won't be disappointed.
Highly recommended, great service and facilities were great. Very accommodating and friendly staff.

A very well maintained park, great amenities and close to lost of outside activities. Lost a star as had an unpleasant confrontation with one of the parks staff who was rude and confrontational.  Out of all the parks we stayed at during our 4 week trip this was the first issue we had unfortunately.
This was our second time staying at the park!

A quiet holiday park which is very neat and tidy. We booked a Sunshine tent with ensuite, which was a very comfy 'camping' option.  However, we had issues with the fridge leaking water. We told staff, and they immediately moved us to a very comfortable cabin instead. Therefore, we were extremely impressed with their understanding and quick response to our needs. The park is situated in a peaceful bushland setting and is very quiet at night. There are a number of activities for children, including a jumping pillow, swimming pool, activity centre, playground and walks. There are a number of ensuite caravan sites and many more self-contained sites. It's only a short drive to the beaches, Trial Bay Gaol, the Lighthouse, shopping centres, and restaurants. There is also an on-site cafe.

I'll definitely be returning before long with my caravan.

Great park for the kids, has everything you could possibly need. We did book because they allowed pets and a few weeks before check in they called to say no pets on school hols which was disappointing but hopefully a once off.

We really enjoyed our stay. We were greated by friendly staff at Recption. Drove through an amazing clean and tidy holiday park. Discovered our beautiful cabin and unpacked and relaxed. There is plenty to do here especially for the kids. Beds were amazingly comfortable with brand new Pillows. We will definitely be returning. Great location! Surrounded by wildlife and great people.

NRMA South West Rocks was a fabulous place to stay for a few days to just chill out.  We had a Boxer, Milo so we were housed in Cabin 10 in the Hideaway Villas which are pet friendly. ❤️🐾
The grounds were kept in immaculate condition as were the cabins.  The staff I did see & spoke to (Dan) was very friendly, welcoming & he actually put new batteries in our remote control for the TV.   A lovely staff member.
A Microwave, 2 induction hot plates, toaster, kettle, fridge so all cooking requirements were catered for as well as crockery cutlery, etc.,   Cabin was excellent with a large comfy bed, a large TV, split air conditioner, a lovely deck with a table & seating outside.  A clothes airer to hang your wet towels on outside in the sun.  Plenty of entertainment for children which we didn’t have but my grandchildren would have loved the attractions specifically catering for them.
Lovely & peaceful area, never heard anything from our Cabin.  I would thoroughly recommend this Park & will give it a 5 star rating.

Great set up for all ages.
Lots to do.
Very clean and friendly staff.
Would definitely come back again.
A beautiful park with good amenities espicially for the kids and great customer service too. Spent a few extra days here due to the floods and the staff at both reception and the cafe were lovely.

Absolutely amazing! Booked the Sunshine tent and our friends booked the other Sunshine tent, one of the tents ensuits had issues so they upgraded both of us for free!
Staff were all so nice and friendly.
Kids enjoyed chocolate bingo, the disco and the water park.
Coffee at the Cafe is 10/10, so good!
Very clean cabins and park facilities.
We highly recommend this place.
Loved it!

What a magnificent park. The facilities, the caravan sites and the staff.
Thank you guys for being so welcoming, especially since we are travelling with our dog. It certainly is a refreshing change from the last NRMA park we stayed at Yamba where we felt alienated because we had a dog.
Keep up the great work because it makes a very noticeable difference to your guests.
Thank you, thank you

Our 3 bed, 2 bath "cabin" was awesome. Only 4 of us but could have slept 8, and immaculately clean!
A beautiful bush setting with lots of activities for those with kids.

Amazing weekend away. The weather was wet, so the activities were limited, but the whole family kept busy the whole time. Rooms were clean and comfortable, with everything we needed. All staff were so polite and helpful. Kids club was really well organised and lots of fun. Convenient location. Cafe on site with pizza/pasta nights was amazing.

We've stayed at quite a few Holiday Parks in the last couple of years and this was by far the absolute best. The service was great and Tracey in guest services was excellent. If possible, I recommend staying in the newer cabins. They are stunning and so comfortable. Thank you for the amazing stay! We'll definitely be back.

Absolutely outstanding. We have just stayed for 8 days. The service from the staff is next to none. The campsite and grounds are immaculately clean. Hot showers and clean toilets 24hrs a day. Kitchen areas are kept pristine. The cafe offers good food for reasonable prices. And the pool area has something for all kids even adults. I would highly recommend and give this campsite 10/10. I will be returning for sure next year.

The park and facilities are lovely as are most of the staff we spoke with during our stay. Although the activities manager was extremely rude and caused tremendous anxiety carrying on over the use of MEDICATION when proved it was medication she did not even attempt to offer an apology for the way she carried on. Do better

Management and reception were amazing , rooms and the whole park were amazing . Unfortunately the activities coordinator was extremely rude and verbally abused and accused my family and the family in cabin 36 .Ruined our holiday completely . Definitely not going back due to one rude worker who was extremely entitled and thought she owned the park.
We come here every year for a couple of days. Little bit far out from Sydney but worth the drive.
Serves us right for going during school holidays.
Awesome location will be back for sure

Stayed on an ensuite site for a week. Very small ensuite and a small shower with shower curtain. Big step up to ensuite. We got a bad site, small, no shade and no privacy. It was a small corner site.|Park is well maintained and clean. Workmen and ladies always busy.|Didn't see any security at night. Lots of kids going around on scooters after dark. |Cafe was great and coffee was the best. |Lots of rules posted around park but no one seemed to take any notice of them, and they weren't reminded by anyone in charge, so annoying.|Heaps of mossies, due to rain, not parks fault but take repellant.|Overall a lovely park, pool was fantastic but spoilt by bad language from some and lack of supervision of kids generally.

Unit 36 water heater malfunctioning||Management aware beforehand||Should never have been rented to us||$470 per night for several cold showers||Date stayed 21st and 22nd feb 2025||Interested to know if anyone had same issue before or after us as ground staff informed they knew it needed replacing

Booked a stay in two bedroom apartment
(4 adults) over 21st and 22nd feb
Unit number was 36
Main issue is water heater continually malfunctioned and several times had to have cold showers
Management of this park knew this heater needed replacing
I would be interested to know if anyone reading this review had the same issue before or after our stay as I believe it’s pretty disgusting to lease an apartment knowing the water heater is faulty

Absolutely wonderful staff, park and facilities. It is so good my child makes it their birthday request to come back each year.
The water park is worth any travel to come to it is amazing !
We live near Wet and Wild but still prefer the water park here as it has so much imagination and fun built in to it . The pirate ship and surrounds make it fantastic fun for all ages .

My family and I were camping at NRMA South West Rocks for a week, the site was lovely, everything you need is provided, but the staff were the difference, they are so kind, friendly and they always keeping everything nice and clean, I definitely recommend.
Great spot at a great price. I camped on an unpowered site and had a perfect spot under a tree and close to all amenities. Pool was awesome and camp kitchen well equipped.

Absolutely loved our time. Planning our return visit for next year.
The pool is amazing! The cabins are clean. The grounds are always clean with staff onsite all day. The facilities are amazing with activities every day. Couldn't have asked for more. Best park we have stayed in.

Great place. Kids loved the water park, Mini golf, arcade, movie under the stars.
Daily activities for kids.
One improvement though, hated walking to reception every time we wanted to do an activity to book. Need some sort of app.

Will be back for sure.

Can not recommend these guys enough!
All the staff were amazing! They were very helpful and polite.
The kids had a great time! (Very tired)
Honestly, the best time!
We will be returning!
Good little find but overpriced compared to other sites, breakfast was good though
Great Caravan Park, fake grass sites are amazing! Top facilities for the kids. Highly recommend 👌
The cafeteria is unexpectedly good. Good coffee and tasty breakfast options at a very reasonable price.

I had a very disappointing experience speaking with TRACY at the reception. She was extremely rude, and as I was speaking, she abruptly hung up on me mid-sentence. To make matters worse, she was unhelpful throughout the conversation. This level of service was completely unacceptable. I’ve stayed at many caravan parks throughout NSW and never have experienced such service.
No grass on caravan stand with limited space for our vehicle. Facilities were poor and dated and not cleaned Properly. Not the December holiday we were hoping for.
Great for families heaps of wild life careful to not leave rubbish or food out overnight as the Roo's will visit. Lots for the kids to do

We stayed in the powered camp sites for a week over Christmas, for a family holiday. This park is amazing, great location and amenities. Park is always very clean and the staff were friendly and kind. I strongly recommend you stay here and the Bear Grylls academy for the kiddies too amazing. Thank you for a fantastic holiday!!

We stayed in the powered camp sites for a week over Christmas, for a family holiday. This park is amazing, great location and amenities. Park is always very clean and the staff were friendly and kind. I strongly recommend you stay here and the Bear Grylls academy for the kiddies too amazing. Thank you for a fantastic holiday!!
Great place to spend the holidays. The water park is sensational.
Absolutely crack’n spot. Great for kids and adults, and has everything you need.  A little oasis in SWR.  Tracey is an absolute champ.

2nd year back here for a holiday with my wife and 2 kids. We love the place tbh, very family orientated, boat loads to do on a physical note both in and out of park just 1 issue we’ve had last time and now is the food side of things. We’ve been to numerous restaurants around town to little pizza joints to any sorts of food places and the most common thing we’ve found was over priced and not the best food. Cafe inside the holiday park has great service however the food is very poor IMO. Burgers aswell as wraps drip a watery oily substance with a foul taste and then food was very dry but dense and just all over the place tbh. Coffee again, not the greatest, banana bread also average at best. If your planning on coming down here, maybe source or plan your feeds out, outside of the food situation the park and what it has to offer itself is spectacular, warm, inviting and very kid friendly. We stayed 3-4 days last time but this time booked a wk as there’s so much to do we ran out of time last time.
Would strongly recommend a visit, would just suggest bringing or buying to make your own food.

So much fun! We had an ensuite site and it was in a great location! Pools is amazing but can be very crowded on weekends- and a little dirty. Too far to walk into town or beach- but with kids, you can really just stay in the park and have fun!

We stayed here for 5 nights during a trip up the east coast. This park was brilliant for our family. A great dog friendly site, extremely clean amenities, a great pool for young and older kids, beautiful wildlife throughout the park, and also the kids activity hub & shaded play area, were soo good! Thanks, we had a great stay!

Booked 2 ensuite sites months ago side by side turned up wasn’t given any instructions on how we should park “our set up” as they require you to park on the concrete and not the grass with the awning opening up on the concrete after we had set up both campers we were advised we had to move everything over a car space as they were “98%” full this weekend and the sites either side of us had bookings so we spent the next 3 hours moving over and to then find out the next afternoon there is no bookings either side of us therefore we didn’t actually have to move over on a extremely hot afternoon! The ensuites are out dated and smell like urine and to top it off the instant hot water stopped working late on Friday night the first night so 3/5 of the family had to have ice cold showers. Absolutely feel we wasted our money getting a ensuite site. Sites are the tiniest sites we have ever seen with no visible clear markers.

We recently stayed there for 5 nights. The park has so much to offer, the staff are very friendly and helpful. The on-site cafe for coffee and something to eat is very reasonable.  It is not too far from other eateries in town. The pools are amazing. There is mini golf, peddle carts, bikes for hire, table tennis, tennis and lots more. The activity hub has something on every day. Saw a lot of children having fun in a safe environment. Wild but somewhat tame kangaroos and beautiful bird life is a bonus. There was a mother duck with 9 ducklings camped near us. It is a caravan park of very high standard.

We didn’t have a very good experience at south west rocks not because of the park but because of our neighbours . We had a powered sight and unfortunately had the rudest , noisiest family next us that was playing loud music , talking and drinking till 4am . After numerous requests from my husband and other neighbours  to be quiet as well as the parks security team . We decided to leave the next day even though we had 3 more nights . We alerted reception on our departure and she apologised and said she would tell management . Well that afternoon we got a call from the manager and she was extremely sorry for our stay . She was so apologetic and felt terrible we cut our stay short that she fully refunded our stay. This to me says a lot about the parks character and we will definitely be back . We understand that it wasn’t the parks fault but to fully refund us and also kick out the family who were disruptive was a A+ for us . Thankyou to nrma south west rocks for looking after your customers .
A good place to stay friendly well priced by the looks licensed Cafe, need to check family friendly and safe not far from Angel rock pools near Trial Bay Gaol...
Best place to go caravaning
Kids had a ball!
Lovely location and great for kids. Sites were large. Unfortunately most parents were not supervising very young children in pool and on communal roads. It is an accident waiting to happen.

Was good for kids but not so much adults first we came to the campground and someone was in our spot as reception had messed it up, we got woken up at 6:50am too kids screaming and people packing up so loud. we left a pair of shoes behind by accident and the people didn’t check for them till I called for a second time so someone had stolen them. a lady on the phone was really rude about it when we called back to ask if they were there and she didn’t have any care. $150 shoes just went missing and they couldn’t help us out at all as people who have worked in that environment our work would have made much more of an effort to find them it’s not hard to ask around or ask staff as staff could have also taken them also. Ended up a very expensive stay We aren’t people to complain at all but this was a terrible experience wouldn’t recommend to anyone.

A wonderful place to stay if you're looking for family fun the location is awesome and you see some beautiful wildlife. there's some really fun activities to do when you're there like mini golf, jumping pillow, waterpark and pool sometimes they do a movie night and a disco. Definitely going back

This caravan park has a lot to offer but we stayed for 5 days and not once were we able to play tennis I don’t understand the need to have to lock the court up and have session times for both tennis and the buggies that only go till 4pm. But other than that this holiday park still remains one of my favourites.
What a great park!!! The amenities are brilliant and so very perfect for kids. Kid and adults LOVED the pool and water slides. Great location close to trial bay !!!
Big camp sites, good pool area, just be prepared for high prices for food etc, better to get supplies from Coles.
It was a nice place & quite, the room amenities was good. And had a relax stay.

Well maintained facilities- SE washrooms near new.|Good communal BBQ / kitchen facilities.|Well manicured grounds.|A few kangaroos early morning and late afternoon.|Really good coffees in the coffee shop.|Very pleasant staff throughout.|Midway between town and the (unfortunately temporarily closed) historic gaol site.|Extensive activities for children.|Extensive pool system seems unheated.|If you don’t like sharing the place with children, avoid stay away for school holidays and probably weekends.

I couldn’t wait to review this fantastic Holiday Park. We stayed for a week in our caravan. Our site was large and shady. The park has so much to offer, the staff are very friendly and helpful. The on-site café for coffee and something to eat is very reasonable.  If you want a park that entertains the little people in your life this is the place to be. The pools are amazing. There is mini golf, peddle carts, bikes for hire and lots more. The activity hub is amazing with something happening every day. Children are very safe here. The wild Kangaroos and beautiful bird life is an added bonus.
We will definitely be back. This is the best Caravan Park we have ever stayed at. Well done NRMA and staff
They had the best cabins we have ever stayed in well worth a visit
Delivery of meals
Love this place. It was winter when we visited. But we will be back in the summer.

We had a wonderful time in the Ngurra Lodge. Kids loved the jumping pillow the most. Received nothing but excellent service from all staff during our stay including the really good cafe on site. Will definitely be staying again.

Jammed in
Everyone has access to this park anyone can get into your camp sites
Great spot but not safe with the local community there and the crime rate is very very high there
Great Park and friendly staff TK's

We have had a fabulous few days here! We stayed at the sunshine tent and loved it! The reception staff was so very friendly and helpful…excellent service! Our kids loved the waterpark even though it was freezing in the middle of July:)) thanks again for a great holiday!!!
Great to holiday here
Beautiful caravan park with lots to do for the kids & at a great price, very happy

Absolutely disgusted with the games room ..the claw machine wouldn’t pick up a feather let alone toys that are too heavy ..absolutely ripped off ..kids very disappointed by this machine AND the go carts are $30 for 45 minute 😳😳😳

We were really excited to stay here. Called and spoke to a lovely lady, she said there were a number of sites available to choose from with our van size / dog and the incoming rain. We agreed that on arrival we would pop into reception and go for a wonder. Upon arriving, after driving 2.5 hours with a one year old, we were told having a dog changed our options (totally fine) and that they needed to seek managers approval (whilst other workers were interacting with our groodle and loving him - one also said surely we can let them in, knowing how friendly he is). They double checked his breed (groodle - the most family friendly doggo). And came back to us saying his too big?! We have never, ever had this issue travelling with our groodle around Australia at pet friendly parks, let alone NRMA. We left really deflated and disappointed, having to get back on the road for another 1.5 hours with our baby to find a secure park before a weekend full of rain. Definitely check the boxes your dog needs to tick before arriving
Fantastic park..Great clean facilities heaps to keep the kids busy as well..
A neat functional caravan park. Perfect stop halfway to Sydney

It was so good. The concrete was a bit of a luxury for us, so thankful for that in the weather we had, rained heavily 3/5 days we were there.|There are lots of camp kitchens and a fairy garden. There is so much to do and so much available to the stayers.|Movie nights, activities for the kids (a mix of free and not-free, but cost next to nothing).|Kart hire and the park is massive that you can see everything in these karts.|There's a games room with some coin-operated arcade games and a MASSIVE in it for indoor movie nights. There are outdoor movie nights, in the good weather, that are projected onto a big whiteboard - the weather wasn't right this trip, but that would have been awesome.|There's an activities hub - you can do putt putt hire and tennis hire out of the hub - and the tennis court is on top of the activities hub.|2 lots of showers and 2 laundries.|There's a cafe with amazing food and yummy slushy cocktails. A mini jetted pool, not heated and a normal big pool.|So much for everyone to do, of all ages.||The wildlife is great too. The kangaroos are everywhere and though I see them where I come from, to see them in the spare camp spots soaking up some sun or seeing them trying to hide under people caravan awnings, just to escape the rain for 5 mins is always cool.||The staff never stopped, there was always someone patrolling the park, keeping an eye on proceedings and making sure the park is kept in great condition.||There is a fire pit next to the center camp kitchen, and the rain didn't stop the stayers from lighting it and having a great get together.||Just wish we had more time here.

So this was our second time here.
The first time was a very brief stay, and we didn't really get to explore the park or the region and we were in a cabin last time.

This time, we had our camper trailer and on a double concrete powered site. It was so good. The concrete was a bit of a luxury for us, so thankful for that in the weather we had, rained heavily 3/5 days we were there.
There are lots of camp kitchens and a fairy garden. A wilderness survival track to explore that runs along the river.
The jumping pillow is always a hit with our 3yo, and the park equipment was great too.
The park itself is amazing! There is so much to do and so much available to the stayers.
Movie nights, activities for the kids (a mix of free and not-free, but cost next to nothing).
Kart hire and the park is massive that you can see everything in these karts.
There's a games room with some coin-operated arcade games and a MASSIVE in it for indoor movie nights. There are outdoor movie nights, in the good weather, that are projected onto a big whiteboard - the weather wasn't right this trip, but that would have been awesome.
There's an activities hub - you can do putt putt hire and tennis hire out of the hub - and the tennis court is on top of the activities hub.
2 lots of showers and 2 laundries.
There's a cafe with amazing food and yummy slushy cocktails. And beer for the men.
Upon our arrival , we got a 2 for 1 coffee card, which was nice to use the morning we checked out.
The Pool is insane, it's huge with a pirates theme swim area. A huge pirates ship, some sea snake sprayers, a big dumping bucket and a skull water feature down a rock wall. A mini jetted pool, not heated and a normal big pool.
So much for everyone to do, of all ages.

The wildlife is great too. The kangaroos are everywhere and though I see them where I come from, to see them in the spare camp spots soaking up some sun or seeing them trying to hide under people caravan awnings, just to escape the rain for 5 mins is always cool.

The staff never stopped, there was always someone patrolling the park, keeping an eye on proceedings and making sure the park is kept in great condition.

There is a fire pit next to the center camp kitchen, and the rain didn't stop the stayers from lighting it and having a great get together.

Thus is a fantastic park to stay at - especially for children - so much for them to do. A great area to swim with a big pirate ship to play on. Tennis court, mini golf, arcade games, bear grills adventure challenge. Awesome. We stayed in a cabin and the bed was comfy and everything else just fine. Pricing was possibly on the higher side but you are getting many more activities than the average caravan park. Highly recommend for families.
Cafe opened at 730 for an early morning coffee
Nice.tidy bcaravan park with its own cafe had great coffee. Kids love the pool and water slide.
Great park for kids, well appointed.
Excellent park with amazing facilities. Close to beach and has great pools and waterslides for kids. Onsite cafe also has good coffee.

Stayed three nights at an unpowered camp site over the Easter Weekend and paid over $500 which is a bit high in my opinion. In saying that, there are a lot of activities, services, food vans, bouncing pillow, pool area, Bear Grylls adventure course, coffee shop and cafe on site plus communal toilets and showers. The pool area needs to be monitored more closely as older children can get rough with younger kids there.  There are a lot of things to see in the area like the Trail Bay jail and beaches with 4wd access. Overall a great experience and we will return in the future.

We were walk ins...well, drive ins to be precise...a last minute turn of the steering wheel, and what a lucky turn it was.
The staff were so helpful and pleasant, found us a site to suit our vehicle, and gave us recommendations on attractions and places of interest...also where to dine out...and I'm so glad we listened to them. The sites were huge and well laid out. As for the park and it's facilities, NRMA South West Rocks Holiday Park
lacked for nothing. It has gained a permanent place on our touring map...We will definitely be back.
Ganze Anlage sehr sauber. Wasserpark ist ein Traum für alle Kinder. Auch Poolanlage ist sehr sauber. Allgemein sehr gepflegte Anlage, schöne Bepflanzungen.

"We recently stayed at NRMA holiday park and had a fantastic time! The rooms were clean, spacious, and well-equipped with everything we needed for a comfortable stay. The park offered a wide range of accommodation options, from cabins to powered sites for caravans, and we found the room we stayed in to be perfect for our needs. The staff were friendly and helpful, ensuring that we had a smooth check-in process and providing us with all the information we needed to make the most of our stay.

The highlight of our trip was definitely the aquatics park for kids. Our children had an absolute blast splashing around in the water, going down the slides, and enjoying all the other fun activities available. The park was well-maintained and supervised, giving us peace of mind as our kids played to their hearts' content. It was great to see such a well-designed and safe aquatic park within the holiday park, making our stay even more enjoyable.

Overall, NRMA holiday park provided us with a wonderful holiday experience. The great rooms and fantastic aquatics park for kids made our stay memorable, and we would highly recommend this park to families looking for a fun and relaxing getaway."
The entire family loved it! Very clean rooms, loads for kids to do, friendly staff, great BBQ areas and camp kitchen. Pool area was amazing for kids. Highly recommend.

Camped here with camper trailer for 2x nights. We had issues with our batteries (in the camper) so NRMA upgraded us to an electric sight for free ! What legends !

Very well kept ! Very clean , friendly staff , if u have kids , there is soooo much for them to do ! I would love to be 10 years old again and stay here !!

Caravan sites look great

Overall just a great park !
Enjoyed our stay here in our caravan.  Coffee shop at the front is way too convenient.  Wonderful for families due to the water park.

While we didn't hate our time at NRMA SWR, we won't be staying here again. |- poor location, not on the beach or any swimmable waterways, 5 mins drive into town. |- poor privacy in cabins, you either need to have your blinds down throughout the day or put up with neighbours being able to see straight through your windows. There is no garden coverage between the older cabins.|- the cabins near the pool are very dated. But still quite spacious and decent layout. |- outdoor setting would be handy|- the little wall mount aircon struggles to heat the entire cabin. Bedrooms can get very hot. |- cabins were fairly dirty. Baby was crawling around on the floor and had black stuff all over knees and clothes. Floor clearly wasn't mopped. We then put a blanket down to play on but it had long black hairs all over it, so they hadn't been washed either. We ended up mopping the floors ourselves. |- we asked for a porta-cot which cost $15 for 4 nights. The bottom of the cot had mould all over it so we didn't use it. |- it's a Mosquito haven, take your aeroguard! ||Negatives out of the way. |- nice big TV in the cabin, and small one in the main room 👍|- has a little Cafe on-site which sells breakfast and lunch (we only had coffees). Which was a little on the pricey side. |- great park for little kids which has a pirate ship pool and 3 water slides. |- tennis court|- outdoor cinema |- games room| - putt putt golf|

An awesome place. We visited her for my friends 50th. The kids had a great time. We stayed in the Eucalypt cottage which was like out own oasis.
The ladies at reception were beautiful.
We are already planning our next trip to you. Thank you!

Do yourself a favour if your heading to soth west rocks book in here. Super friendly and helpful team. The whole park is amazing! awesome amenities and very clean. Kids will have an awesome time with al the activities available here.
Stopped for some information. It looked like a great place to stay for a holiday. Must go there in the future.

This has got to be one of the best glamping locations in NSW. They have so much stuff to do for the kids and the camp-site are large with clean ensuites.

There are options to eat in if you are rushed. The location is quiet and peaceful. The water park is just amazing.
Great for kids. Water part pool area is amazing. Can get hectic sometimes to be expected but! Will be back!
Felt the power an drain are 2 far from van made things difficult

Our family stayed two weeks. During this time, even when it rained we were kept busy with activities. Our children particularly enjoyed the Bear Grylls course. The park is kept clean and tidy, reception staff were friendly and helpful, the kookaburra cabin was spotlessly clean (and cleaning staff were very obliging and friendly). The Pirate ship, along with all the other activities like the carts, jumping pillow and games room were all in excellent condition. Thanks for a fabulous time.
Great facilities! Young kids love every bit and well fitted out for adults too!

We come to this park every year A it is the best kept caravan park in terms of facilities.|Kids love this park. The cabins are new and clean. Coffee shop on premise, Kids club and free tennis and putt golf make it even better. Could not recommend more highly

Large caravan park with massive kids play amenities.
Fantastic for families with young children but noisy for peace and quiet loving couples.

We just finished our 5 night stay with two young children in a Lagoon cabin and cannot speak more highly of our experience. The park is incredible with an endless amount of fun to be had by all. We should have stayed longer and would have if the park hadn’t been fully booked for the holidays. Already planning our next trip back.
Great place for the whole family, offering many things to see and do while you stay .
Nice caravan park that did not disappoint. Stay for longer if you can, there’s lots to do
We had a powered sites for 6 days and we couldn't be happier.It is one of the best parks we have stayed in.

We stayed in a kookaburra cabin which was really nice we had TV and hot water for a shower
Then I booked another night in a kookaburra Villa for $330 a night very disappointed no hot water
No TV RECEPTION and to top it up no ironing board it’s little things that matter.
Also when in the summer leaving ceiling fans on and curtains open a nightmare to cool down.
However not all doom and gloom for kids brilliant park staff were nice.

What a joke NRMA. Thought we might try and sneak away for a few days before the school holidays to save the higher prices in peak time. Tried to book an ensuite caravan site for 4 nights 5th - 9th Dec only to find they want $580 or $522 for members OUTSIDE SCHOOL HOLIDAYS!! Checked right up the coast at other parks and they are all still only charging off-peak prices for those dates. Certainly not a nice way of doing business NRMA!! Just a RIP-OFF!!
Wow, this park has everything. Great for the kids

Stayed for 3 nights on a powered site. Sites are very roomy, all Astro turf with concrete slab for awning/annexe. By far the best park in South West Rocks.  Fabulous family park, the pools are a great hit. The whole place is beautifully maintained & amenities very clean.
Will definitely be back.

Always absolutely love staying at this venue when I go to visit my family or for the weekend away! The rooms are amazing & the service is great too, even when checking in after hours - the process is seamless! Will never not stay here!
Such a great park, lots to do for the kids and so many extra activities set up daily. Best park for kids I've been to yet! Lots of wildlife, clean sites and friendly staff.
Artificial grass is excellent for all van sites and water park is great also.

An incredible park in a beautiful part of the world. The facilities were incredible for our two kids, staff were amazing (even though they were unexpectedly short staffed this weekend) and the location is surrounded by gorgeous day trip opportunities.

It's a great family place to stay.
Pools are great magnet for kids.
The park is well laid out and very well maintained.
We stayed in a cabin that was quite comfortable apart from the toilet/ bathroom being tight.

Powered site, on top of each other. Next camp required to move vehicles to get in and out.
Low water pressure, we hooked into an ensuite site so water pressure dropped when ensuite using water.
Too far to walk into town for meals,  no beach access.
Reception staff very friendly and polite.

If you got kids, or your a big kid at heart this is for you. Great water park, facilities clean. I did find that some guests where a bit too loud after times so my suggestion to the park would be that there is a grounds keeper or something who drives around and says remember to shut up! All facilities great though

Very happy we have picked this caravan park for our spring camping. Clean cabins, amazing facilities, kids club which is open every day. Our kids had a ball there and couldn’t wait to come back. Highly recommended to families with kids. Thank you for the best weekend!

This was a great park. The sites were pretty good size, we stayed on a synthetic grass site with no concrete slab. The pool/waterpark was awesome and my kids absolutely loved it. The staff on check in were really friendly. Amenities were always very clean.

We had an epic week here. Booked it again for next year. Special mention for Karen in the office who sorted out everything for our booking and looked after our group. Already keen for next years trip!

Lovely place to stay especially if you have little kids. Plenty of planned activities and outdoor movies in the evening . Rooms were clean and beds were comfy. Staff were welcoming and helpful. 15 minute walk to o beach.

South West Rocks Holiday Park would have to be on of the best parks I have been to for a break. It is close to township and also walking distance for the fit. To all the staff at this holiday park keep up the good work everyone from reception, cleaning ladies, the garden men, the handymen, the activities ladies, and all their helpers you do an amazing job. We will return to this great place as we like what we saw and the hospitality was excellent.

Excellent  for the kids
Our cabin was extremely  clean & comfortable
thank you
Great for kids and beds were comfy

Great location in the woods, calm and  close to great beaches nearby. Good amenities for spending time with family and kids. Good kids play area, swimming pool and organised kid activities. I recommend.
One of my favorite NRMA parks. Clean and comfortable villa, we stayed in the lagoon cabin. Lots of wildlife around. Great pool, good location

Stayed here for 3 nights in August. Location✔️, Wildlife✔️. This place had all that we wanted for a short getaway. The staff were excellent, special mention to Jo, cleaning staff and Krysta from reception who were welcoming and helpful. They are an asset to your park along with all the other staff. Will definitely be back again.
What an amazing park, something for everyone. Excellent water park and facilities for kids. Loved marshmallows and hot chocolate by the open fire! Will be coming back.
Good place to stay, clean toilet and shower, with a good Camp kitchen

Why is it so hard to see the availability?
Just give me a calendar to see what days are available. Instead of me going day by day to see if something is free.
Wonderful places For a family holiday
we had internet problem in our room. Also, it was problem cellphone in room. (Optus)
Location was awesome as it is located in the middle of all the attractions we loved to see. Everyone is so friendly. Best place that caters for all ages with loads of activities for the kids.
Love our regular visits here! Wonderful staff and amenities!
This is a great park to stay at if you want to stay in a villa. Looks great for the campers as well. Small tennis court, miniature golf great park+pools  will be back here as soon as I can.

I looked at organising a camping trip with a few friends.

Their website claims dog under 18kg are allowed ("allowing small to medium (under 18kgs) well behaved dogs"), however, upon calling to book I was informed that this was incorrect and that dogs needed to be under 15kg with no exceptions. Having a dog that falls between the 15-18kg range means that we were unable to stay here.

I was told part of the reason was animals such as echidnas showing up at the camp site, and that they didn't want to scare these animals off. I have a hard time imagining how a small yappy dog would make a difference in this regard. I agree with large, aggressive dogs being unwelcome around wildlife and children, but at some point these rules become nuanced, especially when our dog has a profile and good history staying at NRMA sites (our preferred choice).

I understand and fully respect the rules. My frustration is around that fact that the planning and organising with friends was done based on incorrect/outdated information on their website. As someone who regularly stays at NRMA sites, this was a disappointing experience.
